anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Dogen's Bendowa practice-realization, especially the claim that training and enlightenment are not two, https://www.abuddhistlibrary.com/Buddhism/C%20-%20Zen/Ancestors/Dogen/Bendowa/Bendowa.htm
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Lowers confidence in the model as a universal explanatory structure. It remains useful as a comparative heuristic, but the anomaly blocks promotion above novel_synthesis until Dogen, Pure Land, Dzogchen, and Christian grace cases are coded without distortion.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: If practice and realization are one, then load-transfer language can create the separation Dogen rejects. Zazen is not simply practitioner agency carrying a burden, nor divine or vow relocation. It is enactment of realization. The ledger can handle this only by adding values such as no-transfer, no-boundary, and analyst-frame-refused. Those values may save accuracy but weaken explanatory compression.

claim_units: The recurring roles are receiving, agency, memory, reflexivity, and integration.
Each role must be coded for magnitude, direction, locus, stage, safeguards, and failure mode.
Load can move from practitioner to teacher, vow, community, text, practice, or divine source only if a compatible capacity or reorientation is present.
The vector directions may be generated by whole-path soteriology rather than by local practice technique alone.
The model should predict cross-training resistance, student failure modes, and bridging practices better than scalar prerequisite models or broad agency-locus typologies.

cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Students or clients with equal baseline ability should show different dropout, alliance rupture, transfer, and relapse patterns depending on whether the method requires self-direction, surrender to process, external scaffolding, memory of rules, reflexive monitoring, or post-session integration. Bridging exercises that reorient the capacity should improve transfer more than simply increasing intensity.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: Replace spiritual capacities with learning or therapeutic capacities: attention, agency, trust, memory, reflexivity, and integration. Code each by magnitude, direction, support locus, stage, safeguard, and failure mode. A change-oriented protocol, acceptance-oriented protocol, exposure protocol, relational repair protocol, and skills curriculum should fail differently when the learner has the wrong direction of capacity rather than merely too little capacity.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: General executive function, motivation, instructor quality, trauma history, or dosage predicts outcomes as well as or better than vector direction, and direction coding has low inter-rater reliability.

exact_claim: A spiritual path requires not only more or less practitioner capacity, but directionally specific capacities. Receiving, agency, memory, reflexivity, and integration should be coded as vector roles with magnitude, direction, locus, stage, safeguards, and failure mode. A path can lower or relocate a load only through a directionally compatible capacity or an explicit reorientation practice. The deeper generator of direction may be the path's account of self, liberation, and what remains after transformation.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Independent coders should infer later manual warnings and safeguards from early claims about self, liberation, and final transformation at above-chance rates. For example, Other Power texts should warn against calculation, practice-realization texts should warn against treating practice as a means to a later goal, and deconstructive paths should warn against reification and dissociation.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Capacity direction is generated by soteriology rather than only by practice technique.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Blind coding trial across SN 45.8, Tannisho, Bendowa, one Dzogchen or Mahamudra manual, one Christian grace manual, and one Sufi fana or baqa source.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Scalar maturity, broad tradition labels, or Pargament-style agency locus predict the same warnings as well as the vector ledger, or coders cannot agree on direction and locus.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Practitioners shifting from effort-heavy practice to surrender, vow, grace, or recognition practice should report specific misfires such as calculation, over-efforting, ownership of insight, or refusal of support. Teachers should prescribe bridging practices that change direction, not merely more practice time.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Reorientation cost is real and not just lack of general maturity.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Structured interviews with dual-trained practitioners in Theravada and Pure Land, Advaita and bhakti, Zen and Dzogchen or Mahamudra, plus teacher reports of student switching failures.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Experienced practitioners switch smoothly when motivation and discipline are high, with no direction-specific friction, or teachers describe failures only as intensity, trauma, poor ethics, or generic immaturity.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Adverse-practice reports should cluster by mismatch type: agency-heavy striving in surrender paths, deconstructive inquiry without integration capacity, devotional dependence without ethical agency, or nondual recognition without memory and stabilization supports.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Vector mismatch predicts adverse practice patterns better than broad practice category alone.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Recode VCE-style meditation challenge reports and practitioner case reports with vector fields, then compare predictive value against existing practice, practitioner, relationship, and context factors.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Practice intensity, psychiatric history, retreat setting, sleep, teacher quality, or social context explain the reports without residual value from vector direction and support locus.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Jodo Shinshu, Dzogchen, Mahamudra, and Christian grace teachers should accept some functional preconditions while objecting to capacity ownership language. Their corrections should cluster around vow-held, teacher-held, grace-held, recognition-event, and analyst-frame-refused values.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Gift and recognition traditions still have observable conditions, but those conditions will not be best described as practitioner-owned capacities.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Practitioner-scholar interviews and manual coding of Shinran, pointing-out instructions, and grace texts.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Teachers either accept ordinary capacity language without reservation, or reject all functional precondition coding while still showing no predictable teaching safeguards.

literature_search_queries: Pargament religion problem-solving process three styles of coping self-directing deferring collaborative

near_neighbors.difference: Pargament studies coping and divine-human problem solving, not a cross-tradition ledger of receiving, agency, memory, reflexivity, and integration across soteriologies.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction. The vector ledger must prove it predicts more than agency locus and divine involvement.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close for locus and direction. Pargament already distinguishes self-directing, deferring, and collaborative religious problem-solving styles by the relationship between person and God, and ties those styles to competence and religiousness.
near_neighbors.source: Pargament et al., Religion and the Problem-Solving Process: Three Styles of Coping, https://icmglt.org/icmlibrary/religion-and-the-problem-solving-process-three-styles-of-coping/

near_neighbors.difference: Engler is mainly psychodynamic and Buddhist practice-facing. He does not supply a comparative vector ledger across grace, vow, nondual recognition, self-power, and practice-realization traditions.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction for the capacity prerequisite claim. Remaining novelty is the multi-locus, multi-direction, cross-tradition rubric.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close for prerequisite capacities, sequencing, and failure modes. Engler explicitly links meditation suitability to ego development, self-observation, affect tolerance, stability, and teacher choice of practice.
near_neighbors.source: Jack Engler, Therapeutic Aims in Psychotherapy and Meditation, https://www.atpweb.org/jtparchive/trps-16-84-01-025.pdf, and Inquiring Mind interview, https://inquiringmind.com/article/0501_1_engler-interview/

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate adds receiving, agency, memory, support locus, safeguards, soteriological direction, and inter-tradition transfer costs.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction for vector-like practice dimensions. Moderate remaining novelty for adding soteriological direction and load relocation.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close. Their attentional, constructive, and deconstructive families already treat practices as training different cognitive and affective mechanisms, with practice positions in a phenomenological matrix and resource bottlenecks.
near_neighbors.source: Dahl, Lutz, and Davidson, Reconstructing and Deconstructing the Self, https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4595910/ and Investigating the Phenomenological Matrix, https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4608430/

near_neighbors.difference: VCE is empirical and Buddhist meditation-focused, while the candidate proposes a comparative soteriological ledger for many kinds of path architecture.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate to high reduction. The candidate should adopt VCE variables instead of reinventing adverse-practice coding.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for failure-mode prediction. The VCE study codes meditation-related challenges by practice factors, practitioner factors, relationships, context, and integration difficulties.
near_neighbors.source: Lindahl et al., The varieties of contemplative experience, PLOS ONE, https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0176239

near_neighbors.difference: Hadot concerns ancient philosophical schools and way-of-life formation, not cross-tradition soteriology or vector load transfer among practitioner, vow, teacher, community, or divine source.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction. The ledger extends a known spiritual-exercise account into a comparative coding tool.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for directed formation. Hadot describes practices of attention, memorization, self-mastery, therapy of passions, duties, and master-student guidance as exercises that re-habituate perception and life.
near_neighbors.source: Pierre Hadot, spiritual exercises, Internet Encyclopedia of Philosophy, https://iep.utm.edu/hadot/

near_neighbors.difference: Ferrer gives a pluralist and participatory theory, not a cell-level rubric for capacities, safeguards, and failure modes.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate to high reduction for the soteriology-level caution. The candidate is novel only if the rubric yields usable predictions.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for the warning that spiritual knowing is participatory, embodied, plural, and not reducible to a single perennial structure.
near_neighbors.source: Jorge Ferrer, The Plurality of Religions and the Spirit of Pluralism, https://digitalcommons.ciis.edu/ijts-transpersonalstudies/vol28/iss1/14/

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate agrees with contextual caution but tries to operationalize it as vector coding rather than stop at critique.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction for the claim that direction comes from tradition. Remaining novelty is practical and predictive, not theoretical.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for anti-generic comparison. Katz and Sharf both pressure the assumption that contemplative states, reports, or capacities can be extracted from doctrinal, ritual, linguistic, and institutional settings.
near_neighbors.source: Robert Sharf, Buddhist Modernism and the Rhetoric of Meditative Experience, https://deepblue.lib.umich.edu/items/25b7e9d4-d1f3-4280-8d53-95fa0b53db5c, and Steven Katz contextualism discussion, https://www.mdpi.com/2077-1444/13/10/983

near_neighbors.difference: Upaya is intra-Buddhist pedagogy and teacher discernment. The vector ledger generalizes the structure across traditions and separates magnitude, direction, locus, stage, safeguards, and failure mode.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction for adaptation-to-capacity. Moderate remaining novelty for the multi-axis comparative tool.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close for matching instruction to practitioner capacity. Upaya already treats teachings as adapted to limited capacities and directed toward transformation.
near_neighbors.source: Buddhist upaya and capacity-adapted teaching, Harvard Divinity Bulletin, https://bulletin.hds.harvard.edu/buddhist-studies-the-buddhist-way/

near_neighbors.difference: Tannisho is one primary path grammar, not a comparative model. It may be better treated as an anomaly than as support.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction and high anomaly pressure. The candidate must avoid recoding gift as practitioner-owned capacity.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close for load relocation and forbidden self-claiming. Tannisho explicitly denies that nembutsu is the practitioner's self-powered practice or good act.
near_neighbors.source: Shinran and Tannisho on Other Power, https://www.themathesontrust.org/papers/fareasternreligions/Tannisho-Unno.pdf and practitioner summary, https://tricycle.org/article/self-power-and-other-power/

next_loop_instructions: Do not publish as a metaphysical claim. Treat it as a comparative and practitioner-facing coding heuristic.
Build the vector ledger as a coding sheet with fields for role, magnitude, direction, locus, stage, safeguard, failure mode, evidence, and analyst-frame refusal.
Add explicit values for practitioner-owned, teacher-held, vow-held, grace-held, community-held, text-held, practice-realization, recognition-event, no-transfer, and undecidable.
Close-read Shinran's Tannisho and letters, SN 45.8, Bendowa or Genjokoan, one Dzogchen or Mahamudra pointing-out manual, one Christian grace text, and one Sufi fana or baqa source before public use.
Test whether soteriology predicts capacity direction by coding self, liberation, and remainder claims before reading later warning sections.
Run an inter-rater trial comparing the vector ledger against scalar capacity, Pargament agency styles, and VCE-style practice, practitioner, relationship, and context variables.
Interview practitioners for concrete student failure cases and repair instructions, not abstract agreement with the model.
Do not raise above novel_synthesis unless the ledger predicts warnings, switching resistance, or adverse-practice patterns better than existing models.
novelty_adjustment: Reduce novelty from 0.70 to about 0.61. The scalar-to-vector move is useful, but its parts are strongly anticipated by Pargament's agency styles, Engler's developmental prerequisites, Dahl and Lutz's meditation mechanism taxonomies, VCE adverse-effect variables, Hadot's spiritual exercises, Ferrer's participatory pluralism, Katz and Sharf's contextualism, Buddhist upaya, and Shinran's Other Power. The remaining contribution is a promising operational synthesis with testable predictions, not a discovered law of spiritual paths.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Practitioners say this is standard upaya, religious coping, contemplative-development theory, spiritual bypassing caution, cultural-linguistic theology, or ordinary lineage pedagogy. They also say the ledger does not change teaching choices or predict failures better than existing teacher judgment.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: Practitioners can use the ledger to predict failure modes, identify hidden reorientation costs, name support locus more accurately, and avoid common cross-tradition mistranslations that older scalar or agency-locus models miss.
questions: Are receiving, agency, memory, reflexivity, and integration separable teaching loads in your tradition, or does that list impose an outside psychology?
Does directionality predict actual sequencing, warnings, and repair instructions for students, or is it obvious under older terms such as upaya, grace, self-power, Other Power, or ego development?
When practitioners move between effort, surrender, vow, recognition, and practice-realization paths, do they need a reorientation practice, or only more maturity and guidance?
Does calling trust, recognition, grace, or practice-realization a capacity distort the tradition's own grammar?
Can the rubric predict a concrete student failure case before you tell us the lineage's usual repair?
relevant_practitioners: Jodo Shinshu ministers and Shinran scholars
Theravada meditation teachers familiar with staged effort and right effort
Soto Zen teachers familiar with Dogen's practice-realization
Dzogchen or Mahamudra teachers who give or study pointing-out instructions
Christian theologians of grace and spiritual directors
Contemplative clinicians familiar with meditation-related adverse effects
Comparative theologians and religious-studies method scholars

primary_pair: SN 45.8, Magga-vibhanga Sutta, translated by Thanissaro Bhikkhu, https://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/sn/sn45/sn45.008.than.html
Tannisho, sections VII to IX, translated by Taitetsu Unno, https://www.themathesontrust.org/papers/fareasternreligions/Tannisho-Unno.pdf
primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. The ledger may turn tradition-specific soteriology into analyst-owned cells. Pure Land entrusting, Dogen practice-realization, and Dzogchen recognition may not be practitioner capacities at all, even when they have observable preconditions.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: The audit searched Pargament, Engler, Hadot, Ferrer, Katz, Sharf, Dahl, Lutz, Davidson, Lindahl, Britton, upaya literature, Tannisho, Bendowa, and SN 45.8. It did not close-read Shinran's letters, Kyogyoshinsho, Longchenpa, Wangchuk Dorje, Christian grace manuals, Sufi fana and baqa texts, or original Pali and Japanese terms. That gap blocks any candidate-discovery claim.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: SN 45.8 reads like a high-agency ledger: right view knows suffering and cessation, right resolve is directed toward renunciation and harmlessness, right effort generates desire and persistence, right mindfulness maintains ardent attention, and right concentration stabilizes the path. Tannisho VIII reverses the ownership grammar: nembutsu is not the practitioner's practice or good act because it is Other Power and free of self-power. Tannisho IX treats lack of joy not as simple incapacity but as blind passion already anticipated by the vow. The pair supports the vector insight because similar path functions are assigned different direction and locus. It also threatens the model because calling entrusting a capacity may already distort Shinran's grammar.

title: The Vector Capacity Ledger
unlike_statement: Unlike Pargament, Engler, Dahl, Hadot, Ferrer, Katz, Sharf, upaya theory, and Shinran, this idea claims that path requirements can be coded as a reusable vector ledger where each functional role has magnitude, direction, locus, stage, safeguards, and failure mode, and that whole-path soteriology may generate the direction of each role. Its possible contribution is predictive rubric design, not the discovery that traditions differ in agency, capacity, or context.
